name: Space Camp
white: Astronaut ice cream, the biggest disappointment in the galaxy.
My homework, floating away forever.
A moon rock that is obviously from the driveway.
Zero-gravity spaghetti night.
A black hole that only eats left socks.
The alien exchange student's lunchbox.
A Mars rover singing "Happy Birthday" to itself.
The sun, the most overrated star in the sky.
An itch in the exact middle of a space suit.
The entire vastness of space, and me, really needing to pee.
Counting down from ten and chickening out at three.
A billionaire's rocket that only goes up for eleven minutes.
The space agency's lost-and-found bin.
Freeze-dried mashed potatoes in a toothpaste tube.
An asteroid the size of a school bus, named Kevin.
A satellite whose only job is watching one specific cow.
Taking the dog for a spacewalk.
A comet that shows up every 76 years just to show off.
An alien tractor beam that only lifts things three inches.
The dark side of the moon, which is just shy.
Saturn's rings, which are basically space hula hoops.
A telescope pointed directly at the neighbors' bird feeder.
A flag planted proudly on the wrong planet.
A hamster in a tiny homemade rocket.
Sneezing inside a space helmet.
Gravity, quitting without notice.
A flying saucer stuck in the car wash.
Docking the spaceship on the fourteenth try.
An alien pen pal who writes only in smells.
Moon buggy donuts in a fresh crater.

black.text: Space camp was canceled this year because of _.

The first words ever spoken on Mars: "_."
The newest planet in the solar system is made entirely of _.
Astronaut training: running, swimming, and eight hours of _.
Day 200 alone on the space station, and I have started talking to _.
The space agency scrubbed the whole mission after somebody brought _ aboard.
In space, no one can hear _. Unfortunately, everyone can hear _.
